title: Support for shared formulae
author: gaberoo
body:
Hi,

From trying to read a file and from looking through the code, it doesn't look like exceljs supports shared formulae:

```xml
     <c r="AE11" s="5">
        <f t="shared" ref="AE11:AE13" si="25">S11-S$10</f>
        <v>2.1</v>
      </c>
```
[...]
```xml
      <c r="AE12" s="5">
        <f t="shared" si="25"/>
        <v>10.2</v>
      </c>
```

Is anyone working on this? If not, I'd be happy to give it a go. Before sifting through the code, maybe you could point me into the right files to add the parsing?

Thanks,
Gaberoo

comments.author: alessandroniciforo
comments.body:
@gaberoo I had exactly the same need and I started working on it. It's just a workaround at this moment. The idea is to copy the f tag attributes upon reading and to write them again upon writing. 
If you wanna check it out: https://github.com/alessandroniciforo/exceljs/commit/a9cdb5c1bbe710ad2dec15779bd69b0e6f5c0300

comments.author: gaberoo
comments.body: Hi @alessandroniciforo, thanks for getting back to me! Hmm... in my case I will need to parse the formula to find what cells it references. But I'll have a look at your modifications. Hopefully it'll point me in the right direction. Thanks!

comments.author: Siemienik
comments.body: @alessandroniciforo @gaberoo currently it should be included in exceljs, Could you check it?
